mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 4.0% signals tightening labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Unemployment rate remains above 4.0% for 3 consecutive months while 20-day SPX returns underperform baseline by >1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates expanding real economic activity that drives corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns are negative in 60%+ of periods where both INDPRO and HOUST show positive momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth acceleration above 3.0% signals strong aggregate demand that increases corporate profit expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns underperform baseline by >2.0% in 40%+ of quarters with GDP growth above 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100 signals weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate earnings forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns are positive in 70%+ of periods where INDPRO declines below 100.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% triggers central bank rate hike expectations that increase equity discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns exceed 1.0% in 50%+ of periods with CPIAUCSL above 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in real sales (RSAFS) combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong consumer and business demand that drives equity valuations higher, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST show positive momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll gain (PAYEMS) above 200k signals robust job market conditions that increase consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns underperform baseline by >1.0% in 40%+ of months with PAYEMS above 200k.
